title: Support $bits() for arrays and array slices

features.name: $bits on array variable identifier returns element width instead of full array width
features.description: Code should include a variable declared via a typedef with an unpacked array dimension, then query $bits() on that variable identifier and on the typedef name separately, exposing the discrepancy where $bits(var) returns only the element width while $bits(Type) returns the correct full bit width

features.name: typedef with unpacked array dimension and $bits on type name
features.description: Code should include a typedef that aliases a scalar type with an unpacked array dimension, then apply $bits() to the typedef name to retrieve the full aggregate bit width
features.snippet:
typedef int T[1:0];
initial $display($bits(T)); // 64: int is 32 bits, array has 2 elements

features.name: $bits on unpacked array slice expression
features.description: Code should include a slice of an unpacked array passed to $bits() to verify that the system function correctly computes the bit width of the sliced sub-array
